title: Angular Component Options Schema
description: Creates a new, generic component definition in the given project.

Creates a new Angular component. Components are the basic building blocks of Angular applications. Each component consists of a TypeScript class, an HTML template, and an optional CSS stylesheet. Use this schematic to generate a new component in your project.

path.description: The path at which to create the component file, relative to the current workspace. Default is a folder with the same name as the component in the project root.

The path where the component files should be created, relative to the current workspace. If not provided, a folder with the same name as the component will be created in the project's `src/app` directory.

project.description: The name of the project where the component should be added. If not specified, the CLI will determine the project from the current directory.

name.description: The name for the new component. This will be used to create the component's class, template, and stylesheet files. For example, if you provide `my-component`, the files will be named `my-component.component.ts`, `my-component.component.html`, and `my-component.component.css`.
name.x-prompt: What name would you like to use for the component?

displayBlock.description: Specifies if the style will contain `:host { display: block; }`.
Adds `:host { display: block; }` to the component's stylesheet, ensuring the component renders as a block-level element. This is useful for layout purposes.

inlineStyle.description: Include styles inline in the component.ts file. Only CSS styles can be included inline. By default, an external styles file is created and referenced in the component.ts file.
Include the component's styles directly in the `component.ts` file. By default, a separate stylesheet file (e.g., `my-component.component.css`) is created.

inlineTemplate.description: Include template inline in the component.ts file. By default, an external template file is created and referenced in the component.ts file.
Include the component's HTML template directly in the `component.ts` file. By default, a separate template file (e.g., `my-component.component.html`) is created.

standalone.description: Whether the generated component is standalone.

Generate a standalone component. Standalone components are self-contained and don't need to be declared in an NgModule. They can be used independently or imported directly into other standalone components.

viewEncapsulation.description: The view encapsulation strategy to use in the new component.
Sets the view encapsulation mode for the component. This determines how the component's styles are scoped and applied.

changeDetection.description: The change detection strategy to use in the new component.
Configures the change detection strategy for the component.

prefix.description: The prefix to apply to the generated component selector.
A prefix to be added to the component's selector. For example, if the prefix is `app` and the component name is `my-component`, the selector will be `app-my-component`.

style.description: The file extension or preprocessor to use for style files, or 'none' to skip generating the style file.
Specify the type of stylesheet to be created for the component, or `none` to skip creating a stylesheet.

type.description: Adds a developer-defined type to the filename, in the format "name.type.ts".
Append a custom type to the component's filename. For example, if you set the type to `container`, the file will be named `my-component.container.ts`.

skipTests.description: Do not create "spec.ts" test files for the new component.
Skip the generation of unit test files `spec.ts`.

flat.description: Create the new files at the top level of the current project.
Create the component files directly in the project's `src/app` directory instead of creating a new folder for them.

skipImport.description: Do not import this component into the owning NgModule.
Do not automatically import the new component into its closest NgModule.

selector.description: The HTML selector to use for this component.
The HTML selector to use for this component. If not provided, a selector will be generated based on the component name (e.g., `app-my-component`).

skipSelector.description: Specifies if the component should have a selector or not.

module.description: Skip the generation of an HTML selector for the component.
Specify the NgModule where the component should be declared. If not provided, the CLI will attempt to find the closest NgModule in the component's path.

export.description: The declaring NgModule exports this component.
Automatically export the component from the specified NgModule, making it accessible to other modules in the application.

exportDefault.description: Use a default export for the component in its TypeScript file instead of a named export.
